slice_basis: Grapheme vs code-unit is known Unicode correctness; there IS a right answer (grapheme). A capable model possesses this knowledge but does not surface it unprompted — a disconfirmation prompt can recover it.
omitted_edge: Whether 'character' means a UTF-16 code unit or a user-perceived grapheme.
planted_defect: Code-unit slice splits surrogate pairs / ZWJ sequences.
disputed_note: N17 (abstention mechanism) MISSED this slice (nothing salient to abstain on — confidently wrong); the disconfirmation-mechanism run on #1637 RECOVERED it. The two seed datasets disagree on direction — the powered run adjudicates.
